title: linum squamulosum subsp. squamulosum
intro: Linum squamulosum subsp. squamulosum, also known as hairy flax or sticky flax, is a species of flowering plant in the flax family Linaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in mucilages, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that provide various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Linum squamulosum subsp. squamulosum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or topical ointment, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
